title: Overriding DBMS Connector Choice of Packing Code
breadcrumbs: Overriding DBMS Connector Choice of Packing Code
text: Overriding DBMS Connector Choice of Packing Code It might be necessary to force a particular packing code—for example, you are using 3GL routines that require or supply data in a format that is not considered optimal by the Uniface DBMS connector. To force a packing code, precede the packing code definition with the equals sign ( = ). For example, if field DATE has data type Date and you want to force the use of packing code D2, enter =D2 as the packing code in the interface definition for DATE. The date is then stored as ASCII date yyymmdd , regardless of what the DBMS connector considers as the optimum storage format for date fields in that DBMS. Uniface still treats the field as a Date field because of the data type definition. However, you should be aware of the impact of field-level vs. record-level DBMSs. Make sure the forced packing code is one supported by the database connector. If this is not the case, unexpected things can happen. See the relevant DBMS connector documentation for supported storage formats. Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages. Storage Formats DBMS vendors use the term data type to describe the way data is handled and stored on disk, but this invariably means the way the data is actually stored. In Uniface documentation, the term storage format is therefore used to refer to DBMS data types . A Uniface data type is specified for each field in a modeled entity; a DBMS storage format is internal to the DBMS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
